articles.full_text:
40 km wind, rain, thunderstorm and…: Delhi, Noida, Ghaziabad, Greater Noida, Gurugram, Faridabad, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan to get…, extreme heat to return on…
Big winds, rain, and storms are coming to Delhi, Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram and nearby areas! Relief from the heat is on the way, but get ready because the extreme temperatures will be back soon. If you live in Delhi-NCR, Haryana, or Rajasthan, you're probably tired of the heat. Good news: things should cool down around May 11. We're looking at rain, cloudy skies, and some pretty strong winds, up to 50 kmph.
Big winds, rain, and storms are coming to Delhi, Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram and nearby areas.
Temperatures to rise first before weather changes
Don't put away the coolers just yet. It's actually going to get hotter before it gets better. Temperatures in places like Greater Noida could hit 33°C by May 10, so expect plenty of sunshine and clear skies for a few more days.
Rain and thunderstorms likely from May 11
The real change starts on May 11. Most of the region will see clouds moving in, followed by some light rain and thunderstorms. It might get a bit dusty too, with gusty winds picking up across North India.
Ghaziabad, Noida and Greater Noida likely to get strongest impact
Noida and Ghaziabad are likely to feel the biggest shift. The thunderstorms will bring a nice break from the humidity and help bring those daytime temperatures down for a bit.
Gurugram, Haryana and Rajasthan also expected to see weather activity
Gurugram and parts of Rajasthan won't be left out either. Expect some dust storms and patchy rain. Forecasters say this is all due to some moisture moving in and shaking things up.
Extreme heat may return after May 13
Enjoy the cool air while it lasts, because it won't be here long. By May 13, the skies will clear up and the heat will start creeping back. We might even be looking at heatwave conditions again by the end of next week.
IMD advises caution during changing weather
The experts are saying we should play it safe. If a storm hits, try to stay indoors and keep yourself hydrated. Watch out for lightning and those sudden dust storms when you're out and about.
